goal: 2026-06-05-session-sources: I3 - Add CLI source option behavior

summary.title: Add CLI source option behavior
summary.what: Added public --source codex handling across the fixed cxs CLI commands, kept omitted source behavior equivalent to Codex, rejected unsupported/non-public source values with a structured unsupported_source error, and threaded the selected source through status/sync/find/list/stats default paths.
summary.why: I3 exposes the source-aware foundation from I1/I2 at the CLI boundary without changing command names or publishing Claude Code support.
summary.user_goal: 2026-06-05-session-sources: I3 - Add CLI source option behavior

decisions.chose: Accept only codex as a public CLI source and reject all other values, including claude-code, with unsupported_source.
decisions.rationale: D1 reserves Claude Code as non-public while allowing Codex to remain the default and only public source.

decisions.chose: Treat omitted --source as codex and canonicalize selector shortcuts with source codex.
decisions.rationale: Existing cxs status/sync/find/read/list/stats workflows must continue without requiring new flags.

decisions.point: Read-only status against old local indexes
decisions.chose: Let status read old Codex index counts without mutating or migrating the DB, and ignore old coverage as not source-aware.
decisions.rationale: status is read-only, and the required checkout smoke exposed an old-schema local DB that should not crash or be migrated by status.

rejected.alternative: Expose or implement Claude Code support through --source claude-code
rejected.reason: This workflow explicitly reserves Claude Code as non-public and assigns docs/skill alignment to I4 only after Codex behavior exists.
The cxs command surface is fixed to status, sync, find, read-range, read-page, list, and stats.

rejected.alternative: Use sync-style migration inside status for old DB schemas
rejected.reason: status must remain read-only; sync remains the only index-writing command.

review_notes: Actual I3 commit is 2ebeb6fe3519097160d7bc01e646f3f6a0fd65b8 on codex/session-sources-i3.
Verification: npm run test -- src/cli.test.ts passed with 27 tests; npm run check passed with 27 test files and 152 tests; git diff --check passed.
Checkout smoke: npm run cxs -- status --source codex --json passed against the Mac default sessions and old local index; temp sync/find/read-page/read-range/list/stats --source codex smoke passed.
Mainline preflight reported expected proposed overlaps with same-workflow D1/I1/I2/control-plane intents and notes_rewrite_drift; classified as stacked workflow overlap, not a semantic conflict.

architectural_claims: The public CLI source boundary accepts codex only; claude-code remains reserved/non-public.
Source selection is threaded through CLI defaults rather than changing command names or positional shapes.
status remains read-only even when encountering old Codex DB schemas.
behavioral_changes: Omitted --source remains equivalent to Codex/default behavior.
Unsupported and non-public source values return unsupported_source with nonzero exit.
Selector JSON source and source-qualified read refs are checked against the public source boundary.
status can read old Codex index counts without crashing on missing source_id.
security_implications: Non-public claude-code source input is rejected before sync/query/read work.
No Claude parser, docs, skill guidance, release, or installed CLI behavior was added.
migration_notes: No new DB migration in I3.
status includes a read-only old-schema fallback for Codex counts and treats old coverage as unavailable rather than source-aware fresh coverage.
